title: Full Regulation-Debt State Machine Features
summary: FIA rules create explicit future stop pressure that simple tyre-age heuristics do not fully encode.
rationale: FIA rules create explicit future stop pressure that simple tyre-age heuristics do not fully encode. The 2025 Monaco three-set rule and the dry-compound requirements create a causal 'compliance debt' that should sharply change next-lap pit propensity, especially late in races or after wet-tyre exemptions disappear.
implementation_hint: For each driver-race, causally track sets used, distinct dry compounds used, whether wet/intermediate usage exempts dry-compound obligations, remaining mandatory dry-compound debt, and Monaco-specific remaining set debt. Convert these into features like mandatory_stops_remaining_min, compliant_if_finish_now, and rule_pressure_now.
expected_effect: Likely most useful on Monaco 2025 and late-race edge cases; could improve generalization on regulation-driven stops that tyre-life proxies miss.
risk: Rules differ by season/race condition; mis-specified state tracking will inject structured label noise.
